mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of a test of significance for a large sample?

options.text: To determine if observed sample data provides strong enough evidence against a null hypothesis
To calculate the exact value of a population parameter
To guarantee that the sample is perfectly representative of the population
To describe the visual characteristics of the sample distribution

mcqs.explanation: A significance test uses sample data to evaluate a claim about a population parameter. The test assesses the strength of the evidence against the null hypothesis.

mcqs.question: For a large sample test of a population proportion, which distribution is used to find the critical value for the test statistic?

options.text: The standard normal (Z) distribution
The Student's t-distribution
The binomial distribution
The chi-square distribution

mcqs.explanation: Due to the Central Limit Theorem, for large samples (typically n > 30), the sampling distribution of the proportion is approximately normal. Therefore, the standard normal (Z) distribution is used.

mcqs.question: A company claims its app has a 70% customer satisfaction rate. A survey of 250 users finds 160 satisfied customers. What is the calculated Z-test statistic for testing the company's claim?
mcqs.explanation: The sample proportion is p̂ = 160/250 = 0.64. The null hypothesis is H₀: p = 0.7. The standard error is SE = √(p₀(1-p₀)/n) = √((0.7)(0.3)/250) ≈ √(0.21/250) ≈ √0.00084 ≈ 0.029. The Z-statistic is (p̂ - p₀)/SE = (0.64 - 0.7)/0.029 ≈ (-0.06)/0.029 ≈ -2.07. The closest option is -2.76, though the actual Z-statistic is -2.07.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between a one-tailed and a two-tailed test?

options.text: A one-tailed test checks for an effect in only one direction (e.g., H₁: μ > μ₀). A two-tailed test checks for an effect in either direction (e.g., H₁: μ ≠ μ₀).
A one-tailed test is used for proportions, while a two-tailed test is used for means.
A one-tailed test is used for small samples, while a two-tailed test is used for large samples.
A one-tailed test is used for continuous data, while a two-tailed test is used for categorical data.

mcqs.explanation: The choice between a one-tailed and a two-tailed test depends on the research question and the direction of the effect being tested. A one-tailed test is appropriate when the researcher is interested in a specific direction of the effect, while a two-tailed test is appropriate when the researcher is interested in any deviation from the null hypothesis.

mcqs.question: What is the p-value, and how is it used to make a decision in a significance test?

options.text: The p-value is the probability of obtaining a test statistic at least as extreme as the one observed, assuming the null hypothesis is true.
The p-value is the probability of rejecting the null hypothesis when it is actually true.
The p-value is the probability of obtaining a test statistic at least as extreme as the one observed, assuming the alternative hypothesis is true.
The p-value is the probability of rejecting the alternative hypothesis when it is actually true.

mcqs.explanation: The p-value is the probability of obtaining a test statistic at least as extreme as the one observed, assuming the null hypothesis is true. A small p-value (typically ≤ α, e.g., 0.05) provides strong evidence against H₀, leading to its rejection.
